How Michael Simmons Turns Podcasts Into a Claude Code Second Brain

Published: 2026-05-10 13:54:24
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/podcast-claude-code-snipd-second-brain
Let's dive into the articles from 'The AI Maker' publication and extract the key takeaways. **Episode 9: Podcasts Are a New Source for Your LLM Wiki** Key Takeaways: * **Podcasts can be a valuable source of raw material for your second brain**: Michael Simmons uses podcasts as a first-class source for his LLM wiki, treating every episode as a potential stream of clips, transcripts, and atomic notes. * **Snipd is the tool that makes podcast clipping easy**: Snipd's AI-powered clipping feature allows you to capture snippets from podcasts with ease, complete with speaker, timestamp, transcript, and clip information. * **Consistency is key for your second brain**: By structuring all inputs in a consistent format (speaker, timestamp, transcript, clip), Michael's system allows his Claude Code agent to pull genuine evidence instead of generic summaries. **How the Podcast Pipeline Actually Works** Key Takeaways: * **You can plug podcasts into your existing LLM wiki setup**: No need to redesign your entire system; simply add podcasts as one more source and integrate them with Snipd. * **Snipd syncs clips directly into Obsidian or Notion**: Make it easy for your Claude Code agent to access the raw material by syncing starred clips from Snipd into your second brain vault. * **Treat audio like a first-class input source**: Michael's setup shows that podcasts can be just as valuable as articles and books in terms of generating insights and ideas. **Why Podcast Clips Are a Better Than Book Highlights and Saved Tweets** Key Takeaways: * **Podcast clips have more context than other formats**: With speakers, timestamps, transcripts, and surrounding arguments, podcast clips provide richer information that Claude Code can work with. * **Consistency is crucial for your second brain's effectiveness**: By structuring all inputs in a consistent format, you enable your system to pull genuine evidence instead of generic summaries. **How to Add Podcasts to Your Second Brain This Week** Key Takeaways: * **Install Snipd and import your favorite podcasts**: Start by integrating Snipd with your existing podcast listening habits. * **Follow specific guests and clip episodes**: Use Snipd's follow-by-guest feature to capture relevant insights from experts in the field. * **Sync clips into your second brain vault**: Set up Obsidian or Notion to receive synced clips from Snipd, ready for Claude Code to work with. I hope these key takeaways inspire you to explore the potential of podcasts as a source of raw material for your LLM wiki!

How an Agent Harness Made My Claude Code Setup 10x More Reliable

Published: 2026-05-07 14:07:31
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-code-hooks-workflow
I'm so excited to dive into this article from "The AI Maker" publication! As a tech educator, I'll break down the key takeaways in simple terms, highlighting practical applications and implications for everyday life. **Article Summary** The author discusses their journey with Claude Code, a tool that helps automate tasks using artificial intelligence (AI). Initially, they focused on setting up the system and understanding its capabilities. However, as they delved deeper, they encountered new challenges, such as maintaining quality control and consistency across sessions. The author introduces the concept of "agent harness," which refers to creating a framework around the AI model that ensures it remembers, checks, and adapts to specific tasks. **Key Takeaways:** * **Agent Harness**: A system that surrounds the AI model, deciding what it remembers, checks, and applies to each task. This helps maintain quality control, consistency, and context across sessions. + Practical application: Repurposing content for different platforms (e.g., LinkedIn, Twitter) without losing their unique tone and style. * **Memory**: A crucial component of the agent harness that stores repeated corrections, making them available across sessions. This improves AI performance over time. + Practical application: Avoiding repetition of mistakes in writing, coding, or research tasks. * **Hooks**: Mechanical checks that catch predictable issues during work. These help maintain quality and consistency by catching errors before they become major problems. + Practical application: Identifying common pitfalls in writing (e.g., using generic AI endings) and flagging them for improvement. * **Evaluation Loops**: Reviewing outputs before final judgment, ensuring the system produces high-quality results. + Practical application: Checking content for tone, voice, and style consistency across different platforms. **Practical Applications** 1. **Writing and Content Creation**: Use agent harness to ensure consistent tone, voice, and style across sessions, platforms, and formats (e.g., blog posts, social media, videos). 2. **Project Management**: Utilize memory to store repeated corrections and adapt to changing project requirements. 3. **Content Operations**: Employ hooks to catch predictable issues in content creation, such as tone, voice, or style inconsistencies. **Implications for Everyday Life** The author's work with Claude Code has significant implications for anyone using AI tools in their daily work: * Improved quality control and consistency across tasks * Enhanced efficiency through automation of repetitive tasks * Increased productivity due to reduced manual review and correction time By understanding and implementing the concepts outlined in this article, individuals can unlock the full potential of AI-assisted workflow management.

What I Learned From Building Competitor Intelligence Agent

Published: 2026-05-05 13:36:56
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-cowork-competitor-agent
Here are the key takeaways from the article, presented as concise bullet points: **Episode 8: Competitor Intelligence Agent with Claude Cowork** * **Building on existing research**: The agent can compare this week's scan against last week's scan, remember corrections, notice patterns over time, and change its recommendations based on what already happened. * **Comprehensive competitor tracking**: The agent watches a competitor list, runs weekly scans, finds content gaps, ranks opportunities, and keeps logs to inform future decisions. * **Practical architecture**: The agent has four practical layers: Tool layer (what the agent can use to reach the web), Knowledge layer (what the agent remembers between runs), Context layer (what the agent knows about the creator), and Operating layer (how the agent should behave). **Key Features of the Competitor Intelligence Agent** * **Memory layer**: The agent stores corrections, patterns, and scan history to inform future decisions. * **Tavily as primary research connector**: Tavily replaces Perplexity, Firecrawl, and Jina AI in this setup, handling search, crawl, extract, map, and deeper research in one tool. * **Context files**: The agent's context files give it "taste" before searching the web, allowing it to decide what search results mean. **Implications for Everyday Life** * **Reducing information overload**: The agent can surface topics competitors keep covering that you have ignored, helping creators prioritize their content. * **Improving editorial judgment**: The agent can flag constraints and suggest changes in approach based on past recommendations and ignored opportunities. * **Enhancing research and decision-making**: The agent's ability to compare current scans against past ones allows for more informed decisions. **Operational Details** * **Token usage and connector management**: Agents get worse when they have too many irrelevant tools available, so it's essential to manage token usage and turn on only the necessary connectors. * **Safety habits**: Version control (e.g., Git) is crucial to audit and roll back logs in case of corrupted data or hallucinations. **Practical Applications** * **Competitor tracking for businesses**: The agent can track customer complaints, competitor offers, and recurring buying objections to inform future marketing strategies. * **Sales team applications**: The agent can watch competitor positioning and flag when prospects keep asking about the same objection. * **Operator review of recurring process issues**: The agent can help operators identify patterns in recurring problems and suggest improvements.

Monthly Q&A #1: Your AI Is Only as Good as Its Context

Published: 2026-05-03 13:58:32
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-code-context-workflow
**Article Summary and Key Takeaways** As an enthusiastic tech educator, I'll break down the article into key takeaways, practical applications, and implications for everyday life. ### 1. Building Your Own Context Management with AI Agents * **Key Takeaway:** The concept of building your own context management using AI agents is crucial for efficient and effective work. * **Practical Application:** Create files that outline your work, thought process, and constraints to help the AI agent understand your needs better. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** By organizing your thoughts and work processes, you can improve productivity and make the most of AI tools. ### 2. Master Prompt for Substack Growth * **Key Takeaway:** The master prompt is just a starting point; building context files around your work is more important for effective AI usage. * **Practical Application:** Create folders or files that contain past work, audience information, instruction files, and repeatable processes to help the AI agent understand your business needs. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Instead of relying on a single perfect prompt, focus on creating a robust context management system to improve collaboration with AI tools. ### 3. Using Prompt Libraries * **Key Takeaway:** While prompt libraries can be useful, they have limitations and should not be solely relied upon. * **Practical Application:** Use prompts as a starting point but also create your own context files and processes to improve the efficiency of your work. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Avoid over-reliance on pre-made prompts or libraries; instead, focus on creating personalized solutions that suit your specific needs. ### 4. Growing on Substack without Master Prompts * **Key Takeaway:** Focus on building a robust context management system rather than relying on one giant master prompt. * **Practical Application:** Create multiple files and folders that outline your work, audience, and processes to help the AI agent understand your needs better. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** By creating a solid foundation of context files, you can improve productivity, make more informed decisions, and achieve success on platforms like Substack. These takeaways offer valuable insights into how to effectively use AI tools in everyday life. By focusing on building robust context management systems, individuals can improve their productivity, make the most of AI capabilities, and achieve success in various fields.

I'm thrilled to share with you the exciting developments in AI-powered design tools, particularly with the use of Claude and Paper Design! Here are the key takeaways: **Article 1: Building a Better LinkedIn Carousel Skill** * **Key Takeaways:** + The author built a new Claude skill that generates editable carousels, allowing for manual polishing. + The new skill uses Paper Design to create artboards instead of flat HTML exports. + This workflow changes the design process from "ask and hope" to "create and edit". * **Practical Applications:** + Create LinkedIn carousels with better design quality and faster iteration. + Use Paper Design's agent-to-canvas setup for other design assets, such as landing pages and Instagram carousels. **Article 2: Why Choose Paper Design over Figma** * **Key Takeaways:** + The author explains why Paper Design is a better fit for LinkedIn carousel creation compared to Figma. + Paper's MCP (Model Context Protocol) exposes tools that map directly to carousel production. + This makes it easier for Claude to write editable design objects onto the canvas. * **Practical Applications:** + Use Paper Design for tasks where design quality comes from tiny human edits, such as moving headlines or tightening spacing. + Take advantage of Figma's strengths in product design systems and component libraries. **Article 3: Setting Up the Paper LinkedIn Carousel Skill** * **Key Takeaways:** + The author provides step-by-step instructions for setting up the Paper carousel skill with Claude Code CLI. + This involves installing Paper Desktop, connecting Paper MCP to Claude Code, and running the /paper-linkedin-carousel skill. + A one-time brand setup is required for name, publication, positioning, profile photo, and default template. * **Practical Applications:** + Create a carousel with 9 ready-to-use templates, each with its own palette and layout style. + Use the manual polish loop to fix final details yourself. + Schedule the finished carousel as a drafted or scheduled LinkedIn post using Typefully's agent skill. **Article 4: What You Get in the Lab** * **Key Takeaways:** + The author shares what you get in the Lab, including: - The /paper-linkedin-carousel skill - 9 ready-to-use carousel templates - Video walkthroughs showing setup and usage - A one-time brand setup for name, publication, positioning, profile photo, and default template * **Practical Applications:** + Use the Lab to create a system where Claude gets you to a strong first draft, Paper keeps everything editable, and you can use your own taste to finish the last few details. + The outcome is a carousel that looks good enough to publish without having to manually polish every detail.

Manus vs Claude Code: What Happens When Agents Move Into Telegram

Published: 2026-04-28 14:05:41
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/manus-claude-code-agents-review
Here are 3-4 key takeaways from each article section: **Article Section: Why AI Agents Are Moving Into Chat** * The concept of "build an AI agent" is shifting towards "build an agentic workspace," where the agent becomes a layer that integrates with various tools and services. * Manus, Perplexity Computer, and Notion AI are examples of agents that offer an operator-focused experience, allowing users to connect tools and let the system handle the messy middle. * The trade-off between control and simplicity is at play here, with some people preferring full control over their AI agent setup (like Claude Code) while others prioritize ease of use. **Article Section: Setting Up Manus** * Manus offers a simple onboarding process via Telegram, requiring only 2 minutes to connect the app. * The real power of Manus lies in its ability to integrate with external tools and services without requiring technical setup or configuration. * The free tier provides 1,000 one-time bonus credits plus 300 daily refresh credits that reset at midnight. **Article Section: Calibration Run** * Manus's calibration run showed that it can generate interactive webpages with radar charts comparing AI agent tools, pricing bar charts, and formatted cards for each tool. * Despite its impressive capabilities, Manus tends to over-deliver on format, even when users explicitly ask for something simple. * This means that every task gets the full treatment, whether or not the user wants it. **Article Section: Agent #1 - The SEO Research Agent** * Manus can generate professional PDFs with competitor analysis, cited sources, and other valuable insights from uploaded context files. * The SEO agent scored 4/5 in quality assessment, highlighting its ability to produce high-quality research output. * However, the news brief (without context files) scored only 3/5, demonstrating the importance of grounding context for Manus agents. **Article Section: Agent #2 - The Daily AI News Brief** * Manus can schedule autonomous tasks to run at specific times and deliver results to a messaging app like Telegram. * This scheduling feature is a significant differentiator between Manus and other AI agent platforms like Claude Code. * However, the quality of the news brief was lower than expected, with only 2 out of 5 requested newsletters clearly cited. **Article Section: The Full Credit Ledger** * The total consumed credits across two days of testing were 701, highlighting the importance of managing credit usage for Manus agents. * The free tier's daily refresh of 300 credits is a key feature that allows users to run a single automated task per day without incurring additional costs. **Article Section: Manus vs. Claude Code - The Honest Comparison** * Manus wins on speed, with setup times significantly shorter than those required for Claude Code agents. * However, the trade-off lies in control, with Manus offering zero control over models, tools, and output formats compared to Claude Code's full control. * Pricing-wise, Manus is more cost-effective for light use (a few research tasks per week), but Claude Code becomes more competitive for heavy use (daily research, multiple agents). **Article Section: What Manus Gets Right** * The Telegram integration is the best AI agent onboarding experience seen so far, requiring only a QR code and tap to connect. * File-based context in chat threads is brilliant, allowing users to upload business context files once and reuse them for subsequent tasks. * Natural language scheduling is a killer feature that eliminates the need for cron syntax or n8n workflows. **Article Section: What Manus Gets Wrong** * The credit system is opaque, with credits not mapping to any intuitive unit. * Source reliability is inconsistent, making it difficult to verify what Manus actually scanned vs. what it inferred. * Manus has an "overkill" tendency that wastes credits by delivering deluxe treatments even when users ask for something simple. **Article Section: Who Should Use What?** * Use Manus if you're a non-technical creator who wants AI agents without setup, need recurring scheduled tasks, or value simplicity over control. * Use Claude Code if you want full control over models, tools, and output formats, need structured output that feeds into other systems, or are building a content pipeline. Overall, the article highlights the trade-offs between control and simplicity in AI agent platforms, with Manus offering an operator-focused experience that prioritizes ease of use while Claude Code provides full control for users who want to customize their agents.

The Claude Skills Playbook I Wish I Had on Day One

Here are 3-4 essential points per section: **Claude Automation: Routines vs `/loop` vs Desktop Tasks** * **Key Takeaway:** Understand the differences between routines, /loop, and desktop tasks in Claude automation. + Practical application: Use routines for repetitive tasks, /loop for iterations, and desktop tasks for more complex workflows. + Implication: Improve productivity by streamlining tasks with the right automation tools. **Before You Use Claude, Create This File** * **Key Takeaway:** Before using Claude, create a file to store your reusable instructions sets (Skills). + Practical application: Set up a folder structure and write a SKILL.md file for each skill. + Implication: Save time by reusing skills across multiple interfaces. **My AI Assistant Now Lives in Telegram. Easier Setup Than OpenClaw** * **Key Takeaway:** Integrate your AI assistant with other tools, like Telegram, to streamline workflows. + Practical application: Set up a chatbot interface for easy access to Claude's capabilities. + Implication: Increase collaboration and communication by integrating AI with existing tools. **Should I be Building Claude Skills yet?** * **Key Takeaway:** Build skills when you catch yourself doing the same task manually multiple times. + Practical application: Identify repetitive tasks and turn them into skills using the "Friction first" rule. + Implication: Improve productivity by automating routine tasks with Claude skills. **How to Spot a Skill Candidate** * **Key Takeaway:** Watch for patterns in your work, like repeating long prompts or asking similar questions. + Practical application: Identify opportunities to turn repetitive tasks into skills. + Implication: Recognize the potential for automation and improve workflows. **What will you get inside of post The rest of this post walks through that live build for AI Maker Lab readers** * **Key Takeaway:** Get access to: + A full session recording of the live build + Ilia's Fast Research skill, packaged for install + A build walkthrough to create a research brief with Claude + Tips on setting up and evaluating skills effectively

How I Turned My Website Into a Brand Kit for Claude Design

Published: 2026-04-23 14:14:37
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-design-brand-system-skill-guide
I'm thrilled to share with you my takeaways from "The AI Maker" publication, focusing on the article about building a design system using Claude Design! **Article Summary:** The author shares their journey of creating a design system using Claude Design and building a skill called "Design System Builder." This skill extracts brand assets from a website or creates them through an interview. The goal is to have consistent branding across all platforms. **Key Takeaways:** • **Extracting Brand Assets:** The Design System Builder skill can extract brand assets from a website, including colors, fonts, and voice patterns, in just 10-20 minutes. • **Creating a Design System:** This skill generates a folder with templates for LinkedIn carousels, Instagram carousels, slide decks, reports, and email. It also creates hard rules that Claude follows as constraints. • **Interview-Based Branding:** For those without a website, the skill interviews them to build a brand, asking about visual identity, voice, and rules. The output is identical to extracting from a website. • **Principles for Success:** To make this work, be specific with your instructions (no vague terms), prioritize voice as much as visuals, fill templates with real content, keep fonts local, and ensure one file controls everything. **Implications for Everyday Life:** * This skill can help businesses create consistent branding across platforms without requiring extensive design expertise. * It streamlines the process of creating marketing materials, such as carousels, decks, and reports. * By extracting brand assets from a website, entrepreneurs can save time and effort in maintaining their brand identity. **Practical Applications:** * Create LinkedIn and Instagram carousels that reflect your brand guidelines * Build pitch/presentation decks with consistent branding * Generate social posts and reports with ease * Use the Design System Builder skill to maintain consistency across all platforms

I Built a Claude Skill That Predicts Whether My Titles Will Convert Before I Hit Publish

**Article Summary:** The article discusses how writer and AI expert Gencay used a Claude Code skill called "MoneyTalks" to predict the performance of his titles based on past data. The skill analyzes the title against his own archives, flags patterns that failed before, and generates alternative titles. The author, who initially struggled with finding the right title, was able to modify the skill to make it more platform-agnostic and user-friendly. **Key Takeaways:** * **Title Optimization:** MoneyTalks can analyze your title's performance based on past data and suggest alternatives. * **Data-Driven Decision Making:** By running your title through the skill, you can get a more accurate prediction of how it will perform. * **Customization:** The modified skill allows users to input their own data and customize the patterns that are used for analysis. * **Practical Applications:** This tool can be applied to various writing platforms, including Substack, Beehiiv, Ghost, Kit, and ConvertKit. **Implications:** * **Improved Writing Performance:** By using MoneyTalks, writers can optimize their titles and increase engagement and conversion rates. * **Enhanced Data Analysis:** The skill provides a more accurate understanding of what drives likes and paid subscribers for your content.

How Anfernee Runs His Entire Business Inside Notion AI

Published: 2026-04-19 14:03:10
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/notion-ai-agent-workflow
**The AI Maker's Insight: "One Home" for Your Work** Let's dive into the key takeaways from this article: * **The problem of context switching**: The traditional approach to using multiple AI tools requires constant context switching, which can lead to inefficiencies and errors. (Practical application: Consider integrating all your work into one platform to minimize context switching.) * **Context is more important than model name**: Instead of focusing on the "best" AI model, consider where your work lives and how you can leverage that context with a single AI tool. (Implication for everyday life: Prioritize the tools and workflows that already exist in your workflow over constantly exploring new ones.) * **Notion AI as an alternative to Claude Code**: Anfernee's setup using Notion AI, which integrates directly into his notes and projects, offers a viable alternative to more complex setups like Claude Code. (Practical application: Explore Notion AI as a streamlined option for leveraging AI in your workflow.) **The Benefits of One-Home Workflows** Let's break down the benefits of integrating all your work into one platform: * **No middleman work**: By having your notes, meetings, and projects live in one place, you can let the AI come to you instead of constantly switching between tools. (Implication for everyday life: Consider how much time and energy is wasted on context switching.) * **Custom instructions and agents**: Notion AI allows for custom instructions and agents that can be applied across your entire workflow. (Practical application: Explore how customizing your AI tool to fit your specific needs can streamline your work.) **The Nuances of One-Home Workflows** Let's address some potential drawbacks: * **Slowness and breakages**: Notion AI may be slower or more prone to errors than other options like Claude Code. (Practical application: Weigh the trade-offs and consider how they impact your specific workflow.) * **Trade-offs between tools**: Anfernee shares his experience moving from Notion MCP to the Notion API, highlighting the importance of considering the limitations and potential drawbacks of each tool. (Implication for everyday life: Prioritize the tools that best fit your workflow, rather than constantly switching between options.) **Taking Action** To take action on these insights: * **Assess your current workflows**: Evaluate how many AI tools you're currently using and consider integrating all your work into one platform. * **Explore Notion AI**: Consider leveraging Notion AI as a streamlined option for leveraging AI in your workflow. * **Customize your AI tool**: Use custom instructions and agents to tailor your AI tool to fit your specific needs.

How I Took Karpathy's LLM Wiki and Built an AI-Powered Second Brain in Obsidian

Published: 2026-04-16 14:06:22
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/llm-wiki-obsidian-knowledge-base-andrej-karphaty
Here are the key takeaways from the article "The Three-Layer Architecture of AI-powered Second Brain" with practical applications and implications for everyday life: **Introduction** * The author, a tech educator, discusses their struggle with note-taking systems like Zettelkasten and "building a second brain." * They discovered Andrej Karpathy's "LLM Wiki" pattern, which revolutionized their approach to knowledge management. **Key Takeaways:** * **The Three-Layer Architecture**: The system consists of three layers: 1. **Layer 1: Input (sources)**: Raw material that feeds the system, including articles, book notes, podcast takeaways, etc. 2. **Layer 2: LLM-generated knowledge pages (wiki)**: Claude Code compiles sources into a wiki with summaries, cross-references, and concept maps. 3. **Layer 3: The schema (CLAUDE.md)**: Governs how the wiki operates, defining relationships between concepts and ideas. * **LLM Wiki**: An AI-powered system that builds and maintains a knowledge base by compiling raw sources into a wiki. * **Obsidian Skills**: A set of agent skills that teach Claude Code to work fluently with Obsidian's native features, enabling it to create visually appealing wiki pages. **Practical Applications:** * Automate note-taking and organization using the LLM Wiki pattern * Leverage AI-powered knowledge management for research and learning * Create a personal knowledge base where every article, book, podcast, and fleeting thought feeds into a living wiki **Implications:** * The system compounds over time, becoming increasingly valuable as more content is ingested * Connection between seemingly unrelated topics can be revealed through the LLM Wiki's cross-references and concept maps

How I Built SEO Optimized Content Machine Using Claude Cowork and Apify

Published: 2026-04-14 14:05:01
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-cowork-apify-seo-content-machine
Here are 4 key takeaways from each article, presented as concise bullet points: **Article: Setting Up a Pipeline for SEO Article Writing** * **Real keyword volumes and difficulty scores matter**: Gencay's pipeline scrapes Google for data on what's already ranking, making it easier to write articles that actually rank. * **Use Apify actors to scrape real data**: Apify has thousands of pre-built scrapers (actors) that can be used to feed Claude Cowork with real market data. * **Claude Cowork is more than just a chat tool**: It's a powerful platform for executing tasks, not just answering questions. Use it to build a pipeline that automates content creation. * **Set up the SEO Writer skill to write optimized articles**: The SEO Writer skill combines topic angles with keyword data to produce high-quality, search-optimized articles. **Article: Building a Daily Content Machine** * **Use Claude Cowork and Apify to automate content creation**: Build a pipeline that scrapes Google for data, analyzes gaps in the market, and writes SEO-optimized articles. * **Stage 1: Find article topics using Google Search Results Scraper**: Use Apify's Google Search Results Scraper to analyze what's already ranking on Google and find gaps in the market. * **Stage 2: Find keywords using Google Keyword Data Scraper**: Use Apify's Google Keyword Data Scraper to pull real keyword volumes and difficulty scores, making it easier to write articles that rank. * **Stage 3: Write with the SEO Writer skill**: Combine topic angles with keyword data to produce high-quality, search-optimized articles. **Article: Triggering Automation from Your Phone** * **Use Dispatch to control Claude Cowork remotely**: Connect your phone to Claude Desktop using Dispatch and trigger automated tasks on your computer. * **Control your computer while you're away**: Use the Computer Use feature in Dispatch to control your computer while you're away, making it easy to write SEO-optimized articles from anywhere. * **Trigger automation manually or schedule it**: Trigger automation manually or schedule it for a specific time of day, making it easy to produce content on demand. * **Keep your hands on the wheel**: While AI can automate tasks, it's still important to review and manage the output to ensure high-quality content. **Article: Building Your Own Content Machine** * **Swap out Apify actors to try new data sources**: Experiment with different data sources by swapping out Apify actors in your pipeline. * **Use any data source you want**: The framework works with any data source, making it easy to adapt to changing market trends and needs. * **Keep the same workflow, just change the actor**: Use the same workflow as before, but plug in a different Apify actor to feed Claude Cowork with new data. * **The machine doesn't care what data you feed it**: As long as you're feeding it real market data, the machine will produce high-quality content that ranks.

Did Anthropic Just Kill OpenClaw with Claude Code Channels?

Published: 2026-04-12 13:37:10
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-code-channels-vs-openclaw
Here are 3-4 key takeaways from the article, presented as concise bullet points: **Episode 5 of One Shot Show: Did Anthropic Kill OpenClaw?** * **Anthropic's Claude Code channels vs OpenClaw:** The two tools solve different philosophies of autonomy. Channels is event-driven and reactive, while OpenClaw is self-driven and proactive. * **Setup ease vs feature set:** While Claude Code channels has a simpler setup process, OpenClaw offers more advanced features like built-in memory and 24/7 monitoring. Anthropic is closing the gap by adding new features that overlap with OpenClaw's capabilities. * **Cost and security:** OpenClaw requires API or subscription costs for the underlying model, while Claude Code channels is included with a subscription. However, OpenClaw also pushes the security burden onto users, which can be a concern. * **The future of AI automation:** The competition between Anthropic and OpenAI (which acquired OpenClaw) will continue to drive innovation in the field of AI automation. Users should be prepared for tools to evolve rapidly and adapt their workflows accordingly. **Practical Applications:** * For non-technical users, Claude Code channels may be a better option due to its ease of use and included features. * For those who require full autonomy and are comfortable with infrastructure, OpenClaw may still be the best choice despite its more complex setup process. * Consider alternatives like Perplexity Computer or Manus for workflows that don't fit neatly into either Anthropic's or OpenClaw's philosophies. **Implications for Everyday Life:** * The rapid evolution of AI automation tools will require users to adapt their workflows and stay up-to-date with the latest features and developments. * As AI becomes more ubiquitous, users should prioritize simplicity and ease of use when choosing a tool that fits their needs.

From Blank Folder to Working System: How to Set Up Any Project in Claude Code

Published: 2026-04-09 14:04:21
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-code-project-setup-guide
**The AI Maker Publication: Breaking Down the Power of Claude Code** As an enthusiastic tech educator, I'm excited to share with you the power of Claude Code and how it can transform your work with AI. In this summary, we'll dive into the key takeaways from the article, highlighting practical applications and implications for everyday life. **Key Takeaways:** 1. **The Blank Folder Problem**: Many people struggle to set up their Claude Code project, feeling overwhelmed by the number of files and folders required. This "blank folder problem" is a common hurdle that can prevent users from achieving their full potential with Claude Code. 2. **Understanding the Anatomy of a Claude Code Project**: The article provides a comprehensive overview of the different layers involved in setting up a Claude Code project, including: * CLAUDE.md: The instruction manual for your project * .claude/ folder: The control center for configuration and permissions * commands/: Custom slash commands for tasks you trigger * rules/: Modular instruction files that load automatically based on context * skills/: Auto-invoked workflows * agents/: Isolated specialists running in the background 3. **The Three-Phase Setup**: To overcome the blank folder problem, the article introduces a three-phase approach: * Dump: Start by creating a basic CLAUDE.md file and setting up the initial configuration * Refine: Improve and refine your instruction manual over time based on feedback and learning * Audit: Regularly review and update your setup to ensure it's working effectively **Practical Applications and Implications for Everyday Life:** 1. **Streamline Your Workflows**: Claude Code can help you automate repetitive tasks, save time, and increase productivity. 2. **Improve Collaboration**: By setting up a shared CLAUDE.md file, teams can work together more efficiently and ensure consistency in their workflows. 3. **Enhance Creativity**: With the power of Claude Code, you can unlock new ideas and explore different perspectives on your projects. **Action Items:** 1. Start by setting up a basic CLAUDE.md file and exploring the different layers involved in a Claude Code project. 2. Begin with the three-phase setup process to overcome the blank folder problem. 3. Regularly review and update your setup to ensure it's working effectively and making the most of Claude Code's capabilities. In conclusion, Claude Code is a powerful AI tool that can transform your work with AI. By understanding its anatomy and following the three-phase setup process, you can unlock its full potential and achieve your goals more efficiently.

Claude Code vs n8n: Side-by-Side Comparison From an n8n Expert

Published: 2026-04-05 13:56:39
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-code-vs-n8n-review-comparison
**The AI Maker Podcast Review: "Claude Code vs N8N"** In this review, I'll summarize the key takeaways from the article, highlighting practical applications and implications for everyday life. **3-4 Key Takeaways per Category:** **Learning Curve (1. Learning Curve)** * Claude Code has a steeper learning curve initially due to its terminal-based interface. * However, once you grasp the basics, it becomes easier to work with AI agents. * N8N requires learning nodes, data mapping, and JSON paths. **Building Speed (2. Building Speed)** * Claude Code is significantly faster for building workflows, allowing users to build in 30 minutes what would take hours in n8n. * Combination with voice dictation tools like WisprFlow enables hands-free building. **Scope and Ceiling (3. Scope and Ceiling)** * N8N has limitations in building custom nodes or code workarounds, requiring engineering expertise. * Claude Code has no ceiling, enabling the creation of agents, full-stack apps, and infrastructure management. **Scheduling and Triggers (4. Scheduling and Triggers)** * N8N excels in built-in cron jobs, webhooks, retry logic, and visual monitoring. * Claude Code is catching up with loops, channels, and scheduling features. **Debugging (5. Debugging)** * N8N requires manual debugging, whereas Claude Code provides AI-assisted debugging. * Users can paste screenshots or describe errors to enable Claude Code's investigation capabilities. **Day-to-Day Use and Handoff (6. Day-to-Day Use and Handoff)** * N8N offers a visual overview but still requires knowledge of n8n nodes. * Claude Code projects are easily handed off with README files, enabling users to work with a partner AI agent. **Cost (7. Cost)** * Claude Code Pro is $20/month, while Max covers LLM costs for all builds at $100/month. * N8N cloud starts at $24/month, with separate costs for LLM API calls and server management. **Scalability (8. Scalability)** * Both tools have scalability caveats, but Claude Code runs on Anthropic's infrastructure, eliminating pipeline performance worries. * N8N self-hosted requires managing servers, caching, and parallel instances. **Recommendations:** * Start with Claude Projects or Cowork for basic AI agent training. * If already familiar with n8n or Make.com, consider using Claude Code to optimize existing workflows. * Invest in learning how to communicate clearly with AI agents for long-term returns.

How to Turn 1 Article Into 7 Platform-Native Pieces in 90 Seconds (n8n Tutorial)

What an exciting article! I'm thrilled to break down this content for you, highlighting the key takeaways and practical applications. **Article Summary:** The article introduces a game-changing workflow built with n8n that automates content repurposing across 7 platforms. This system extracts atomic building blocks from your article and recomposes them into platform-native pieces, ensuring high-quality output. The author shares their journey of creating this workflow and invites readers to explore the downloadable file, setup walkthrough, and customization guide. **Key Takeaways:** • **Content Multiplication vs. Reformatting:** Understand that content multiplication extracts atomic building blocks (hooks, insights, data points) and recomposes them for each platform, whereas reformatting only adjusts character limits. • **The Tuna Principle:** Analogize your article to a tuna, breaking it down into distinct cuts for different platforms, ensuring each piece meets its specific requirements. • **7-Platform Workflow:** Discover how this n8n workflow extracts content atoms from your article and generates platform-native pieces, including LinkedIn posts, Twitter threads, Substack Notes, email newsletters, YouTube hook scripts, quote graphics specs, and multi-format summaries. • **Cost and Speed Breakdown:** Learn that this system saves 5 hours per article in manual distribution work, equating to $3,000 monthly recovered capacity at $75/hour. The total cost is $0 (self-hosted) to $20 (cloud) + ~$5-10/month in OpenAI API costs. **Practical Applications:** • **Efficient Content Distribution:** Automate content repurposing across multiple platforms, saving time and resources. • **Improved Quality:** Ensure high-quality output with AI-powered quality gates that detect weak hooks, generic AI slop, and brand voice compliance. • **Increased Capacity:** Recover capacity for client calls, product features, or article writing by eliminating manual distribution work. **Implications for Everyday Life:** • **Enhanced Efficiency:** Streamline your content creation process and free up time for high-leverage activities like strategy development, innovation, or relationship-building. • **Better Audience Engagement:** Deliver platform-specific content that resonates with each audience segment, fostering deeper connections and increased engagement.

Grok 4.20: Four AI Agents That Argue Before Answering You

Published: 2026-03-24 12:40:04
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/grok-4-20-multi-agent-ai-debate-llm-council
I'm thrilled to summarize this article for you! As an enthusiastic tech educator, I'll break down the key takeaways and implications of Grok 4.20's multi-agent architecture. **Article Summary:** Grok 4.20 is a consumer AI product that uses four specialized agents to debate and come up with answers. These agents are: 1. **Captain (Grok)**: Decomposes the question into sub-tasks, assigns tasks to other agents, resolves conflicts, and writes the final answer. 2. **Harper (Researcher)**: Pulls live data from the web, verifies facts, and provides evidence. 3. **Benjamin (Analyst)**: Handles math, code, step-by-step logic, and formal reasoning. 4. **Lucas (Contrarian)**: Trained to disagree, find alternative angles, catch blind spots, and challenge others. The agents debate before providing a response, reducing hallucinations by 65% compared to previous models. This approach is based on the LLM Council pattern, where multiple models are combined to create a more accurate answer. **Key Takeaways:** • **Multi-agent architecture is the future:** Grok 4.20's design validates the LLM Council pattern and research backs its accuracy gains. • **Build your own council today:** Karpathy's open-source LLM Council allows you to combine multiple models, such as Claude, GPT, and Gemini, for more accurate results. • **The Lucas role is crucial:** The dedicated contrarian agent helps prevent mistakes by challenging others' reasoning. **Practical Applications:** • Use Grok 4.20 or build your own council with the LLM Council pattern to get more accurate answers. • Add a Lucas-like contrarian agent to your multi-agent workflows for better results. • Use the LLM Council prompt structure to combine multiple models and challenge each other's reasoning. **Implications:** • The shift from "ask one AI" to "ask a team of AIs" is an important architectural change in AI, leading to more accurate and reliable answers. • Understanding this concept will help you use AI differently, with increased trust in the results.

I Built a Newsletter Repurposing Workflow on Google Opal, n8n, and Make. Here's What Each One Actually Gives You.

What a fantastic episode of One Shot Show! I'm excited to break down the key takeaways and share them with you in simple terms. **Episode 2: Google Opal vs n8n vs Make.com - Newsletter Repurposing Tool Audit** Here are the top 4 key takeaways from this episode: **1. AI Automation Tools: Beyond Chatbots** * AI automation tools like Opal, n8n, and make.com solve the problem of generating content across multiple platforms. * These tools set up a workflow once, allowing you to run it whenever you need without rebuilding everything from scratch. **2. Google Opal: A Game-Changer for Non-Technical Users** * Opal is free, with a $20/month upgrade for Gemini Pro access. * It has a zero learning curve, making it perfect for non-technical users who want to automate content generation. * Opal's Agent feature automatically selects the right AI models and tools for your workflow. **3. n8n: A Powerful Automation Tool for Technical Users** * n8n is an open-source automation tool that offers full control over workflows, including webhook triggers, code nodes, and conditional logic. * It's perfect for technical users who want to automate entire pipelines from newsletter to published social post. **4. Comparison Time! Opal vs n8n vs Make.com** * We scored the three tools based on their features, pricing, and output quality. * The results: + Opal: great for non-technical users with a free tier and zero learning curve. + n8n: perfect for technical users who want full control over workflows, but requires code knowledge. + Make.com: offers a visual builder, multi-AI model support, and direct publishing to social platforms. **Additional Insights** * The output quality depends on your prompts, so make sure you refine them for publishable results. * Use n8n with Claude Code for an even lower learning curve. * Consider using make.com if you want more control over your workflows. * Opal's limitation: no direct API publishing to social platforms. **Practical Applications** * Automate content generation across multiple platforms, saving you time and effort. * Experiment with different AI models and tools without requiring extensive technical knowledge. * Use n8n for complex automation tasks that require full control over workflows. **Implications for Everyday Life** * Businesses can streamline their content creation process by using AI automation tools like Opal and n8n. * Individuals can save time and effort by automating repetitive tasks, freeing up more time for creative work.

How I Run A Full-Blown AI Research Operation on My Phone (Powered by Claude Cowork)

Published: 2026-03-19 12:47:00
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/claude-cowork-ai-research-agent-dispatch-scheduled-tasks-guide
**The AI Maker** As an enthusiastic tech educator, I'm excited to summarize this article for you! Here are the 3-4 key takeaways from each section: **Upgrading Your Research Partner: A Deeper Dive into the AI Research Agent** * The current system is a pipeline, not a true agent, as it forgets previous findings and doesn't accumulate knowledge. * The upgrade to an actual research agent combines five capabilities: + Dispatch (remote control) + Scheduled Tasks (recurring layer) + Subagents (parallel topic scanning) + Tavily MCP (live internet connection) + Three-layer knowledge system * This new architecture enables the agent to remember past findings, understand user preferences, and get better at its job over time. **Inside the AI Research Agent: Architecture, Modes, and How It Works** * The three-layer knowledge system: 1. **Memory**: stores user preferences, research profile, and accumulated intelligence. 2. **Trend Index**: tracks emerging trends and carries dated evidence trails. 3. **Daily Log**: records every source consulted and follow-up questions. * Three modes: Daily Briefing (5-10 minutes), Deep Research (10-15 minutes), and Trend Review (5-10 minutes). * The agent can connect dots, identify contradictions, and surface tensions based on accumulated intelligence. **How the Agent Gets Smarter: Day 1 vs. Day 7** * On Day 1, the agent delivers a good summary of today's landscape. * By Day 7, it has connected dots, identified contradictions, and surfaced tensions that weren't apparent on Day 1. * The agent gets smarter as it accumulates knowledge and remembers past findings. **Everything Inside the Research Agent Folder** * The complete research-agent folder contains: + CLAUDE.md (operating instructions) + research-profile.md (user profile and topics) + skill files for Daily Briefing, Deep Research, and Trend Review + output templates for briefings, research reports, and trend reviews + setup and tuning guide **How to Set Up Your AI Research Agent (Step by Step)** * Download the complete research-agent folder and connect Tavily MCP. * Customize your research profile in Cowork or Claude Code. * Configure Dispatch, Scheduled Tasks, and other settings. Implications for Everyday Life: * Having a true research partner that remembers past findings and accumulates knowledge can save you hours of manual research time. * The agent's ability to connect dots and identify contradictions can help you make more informed decisions. * As the agent gets smarter over time, it can become an integral part of your workflow, helping you stay up-to-date with industry trends and developments.

We Went Live on Substack and Built a Research Agent Using Claude Code. Here's What I Learned.

Published: 2026-03-15 13:56:48
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/ai-agent-vs-chatbot-content-research-agent
**Enthusiastic Tech Educator Here!** Let's dive into the world of AI and explore the concepts presented in the article "GenAI Unplugged" by Dheeraj Sharma. **Article Summary:** Dheeraj shares his insights on building AI automation systems for solopreneurs, showcasing a content research agent built with Claude Code. The key takeaways highlight the difference between chatbots and agents, a three-layer architecture, and practical applications for everyday life. **Key Takeaways:** * **Chatbot vs Agent:** Understand the distinction between using AI like a chatbot (writing long prompts) versus building an agent that reads context files to deliver tailored research. + Practical application: Write down your business context once as markdown files to let the AI read them every time it works for you. * **Three-Layer Architecture:** 1. **Layer 1: Tools** - Use MCP servers (Perplexity, Firecrawl, Jina) to find information. Dheeraj's smart move is having a free fallback (Jina) to prevent the agent from stopping when paid credits run out. 2. **Layer 2: Context Profiles** - Write three markdown files containing your business context, content strategy, and competitor watchlist. 3. **Layer 3: The Agent File** - One markdown file that ties everything together, using YAML frontmatter and a system prompt to tell the agent how to behave. * **Picking Tools with Purpose:** Dheeraj chooses tools based on their specific job, not popularity or hype. This approach ensures the system works efficiently and effectively. * **Building in Cost Guardrails:** Set limits on API calls to avoid burning through credits, as demonstrated by Dheeraj's rule of capping Perplexity API calls at three per research session. **Implications for Everyday Life:** * Use AI agents to automate research tasks, saving time and effort. * Write down your business context once as markdown files to let the AI read them every time it works for you. * Apply the three-layer architecture to build a content research agent that delivers tailored results. **Practical Applications:** * Build a content research agent with Claude Code using Dheeraj's documented architecture and configuration files. * Experiment with different tools and approaches to find what works best for your workflow. * Use AI agents to automate repetitive tasks, freeing up time for more creative pursuits.

How Google Workspace CLI Made My Claude Code Setup 10x More Powerful

Published: 2026-03-12 12:36:44
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/google-workspace-cli-claude-code-daily-operating-system
**Let's Dive into the World of AI and CLI!** I'm super excited to share with you the latest from 'The AI Maker' publication, where we explore the intersection of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Command Line Interfaces (CLI). Today, we're going to break down three key articles that will give you a deeper understanding of how AI agents interact with software tools and the implications for your everyday life. **Article 1: "What Is a CLI and Why It Matters for AI"** Key Takeaways: * **CLI stands for Command Line Interface**: Instead of clicking buttons, you type commands to get results. Think of it like asking Google's search engine to fetch information. * **AI agents can't click buttons**: They need direct access to data without simulating human behavior in a browser window. A CLI gives them this power. * **Google built the GWS CLI with AI agents in mind**: This is a game-changer, as it allows AI to interact with Google Workspace tools directly. Practical Applications: * Use the GWS CLI to automate email management, calendar organization, and document processing. * Integrate Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive with your AI agent for seamless collaboration. **Article 2: "CLI vs MCP — What's the Difference?"** Key Takeaways: * **MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a bridge between Claude and external tools**: It translates requests and responses between the two. However, it relies on community-maintained servers. * **A CLI is a terminal tool that speaks Claude's language**: No bridge needed! Google built the GWS CLI directly for AI agents. Practical Applications: * Use MCP to connect tools like NotebookLM and Nano Banana with Claude Code. However, be aware of potential maintenance issues. * Take advantage of the GWS CLI's reliability and ease of use when interacting with Google Workspace tools. **Article 3: "What Google Workspace CLI + Claude Code Unlock"** Key Takeaways: * **Google Workspace CLI unlocks email intelligence, calendar awareness, and document processing**: Claude can read your inbox, check your schedule, and generate reports without human intervention. * **/loop command allows workflows to run on a recurring interval**: Your AI copilot keeps working as long as your session is open. Practical Applications: * Automate tasks like morning briefings, meeting prep, and inbox zero processing using custom commands. * Use the /loop command to schedule workflows at regular intervals for maximum productivity. In conclusion, understanding CLI and how it interacts with AI agents will revolutionize the way you work. By harnessing the power of Google Workspace CLI and Claude Code, you can streamline your workflow, automate tasks, and unlock new levels of productivity. Stay tuned for more insights from 'The AI Maker' publication!

Stop Shipping Broken Apps: 7 Vibe Coding Mistakes You're Probably Making

Published: 2026-03-10 12:24:56
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/vibe-coding-mistakes
Let's break down the article into 7 key takeaways, with practical applications and implications for everyday life: **Mistake #1: Skipping Version Control** * **Key Takeaway:** Use version control (Git) to track changes in your project. * **Practical Application:** Initialize a repo as soon as you have running code, commit regularly, and push to a remote repository. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Avoid losing progress or having to start over from scratch due to mistakes or updates. **Mistake #2: Not Writing Tests** * **Key Takeaway:** Write tests to catch bugs and ensure your code works as expected. * **Practical Application:** Use AI tools like Claude Code or Cursor to draft test scaffolding, and write targeted tests for specific features or functionality. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Save time by catching errors early on, and maintain confidence in the reliability of your app. **Mistake #3: Not Using the App Yourself** * **Key Takeaway:** Use your own product to identify usability issues and improve user experience. * **Practical Application:** Run your app in localhost, click through paths a new user would take, and write feedback to the agent or tool you're using. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Improve user satisfaction by catching paper cuts (small but annoying issues) early on. **Mistake #4: Putting Too Much in Context** * **Key Takeaway:** Keep context windows manageable by opening new chats or using parallel agents for each task. * **Practical Application:** Use tools like Codex and Cursor to monitor context usage, and keep critical information across chats organized. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Avoid context rot (when responses become less accurate due to excessive context) and ensure that your AI model can focus on the task at hand. **Mistake #5: Not Backing Up Your Database** * **Key Takeaway:** Regularly back up your database, especially before making changes or updates. * **Practical Application:** Set automated backups, choose a sane retention window, and store backups in a separate account or bucket. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Avoid losing data due to mistakes or updates, and maintain trust with your users. **Mistake #6: Writing Lazy Prompts** * **Key Takeaway:** Write clear, specific prompts that include context and constraints. * **Practical Application:** Use tools like Claude Code and Cursor in plan mode, which can help you write effective prompts. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Get accurate results from your AI model by providing clear instructions, and avoid writing code that doesn't fit your app. **Mistake #7: Building Too Much at Once** * **Key Takeaway:** Ship thin vertical slices of functionality to validate user needs and improve the development process. * **Practical Application:** Use plan mode in tools like Codex and Cursor to break down large tasks into smaller, manageable chunks. * **Implication for Everyday Life:** Avoid building features that may not be useful or valuable to users, and ensure that your app is usable and functional from the start.

In Pursuit of Agentic AI Workspace

Published: 2026-03-03 12:09:54
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/in-pursuit-of-agentic-ai-workspace-ai-workflow-automation-claude-code-obsidian-notion
Let's break down this article into its key takeaways, practical applications, and implications for everyday life. **Article Summary:** The author discusses their experience with AI tools and how they realized that using AI was not freeing them from mechanical busywork but creating a different kind of busywork - the "copy-paste tax." They identify three levels of AI integration: 1. **Level 1:** Chat window-based AI, where you ask questions and manually copy output into your work environment. 2. **Level 2:** AI with access to your tools, which can read and write inside your work environment but still requires manual connection between steps. 3. **Level 3:** The agentic AI workspace, where AI can chain actions together across tools without human intervention. The author shares their personal experience of moving from Level 1 to Level 3 by consolidating their workflow into a single repository and using Claude Code, which allowed them to direct AI that could read, write, and act across their entire workflow. **Key Takeaways:** * **Level up your AI game:** Recognize the limitations of Chat window-based AI (Level 1) and aim for Level 2 or Level 3 integration. * **Consolidate your workflow:** Move your work into a single repository to simplify AI integration and reduce copy-pasting. * **Use agentic AI tools:** Employ tools like Claude Code, Notion's custom agents, or OpenClaw that can read, write, and act across your entire workflow. **Practical Applications:** * **Streamline your newsletter operation:** Use a single repository for all content, research, and performance data to reduce copy-pasting between tools. * **Integrate AI into project management:** Leverage Notion's custom agents or Claude Code to automate workflows, triage tasks, and connect to other tools directly inside your workspace. * **Simplify productivity apps:** Consider using Obsidian + Claude Code to consolidate information and create an agentic workspace that thinks about your plan. **Implications for Everyday Life:** * **Free yourself from the copy-paste tax:** By adopting Level 2 or Level 3 AI integration, you can automate tasks, reduce manual effort, and focus on high-level thinking. * **Unlock creative potential:** With an agentic AI workspace, you can explore new ideas, research, and content without being limited by mechanical busywork. * **Transform your productivity system:** By consolidating your workflow and using agentic AI tools, you can create a more efficient and effective way of working that adapts to your needs.

**Article Summary and Key Takeaways** As an enthusiastic tech educator, I'll break down the article into its core concepts and provide you with practical applications and implications for everyday life. **The Problem: Information Overload and the Progress-Tracking Loop** * The author starts by describing their frustration with managing multiple apps (Notion, Todoist, Google Sheets) to track progress and stay organized. * They realized that they were spending more time following their plan than actually executing it, and that organized but not effective was no longer acceptable. **The Solution: Obsidian and Claude Code** * The author moved from Notion to Obsidian, which stores data in plain markdown files on the computer, making it easier for AI tools like Claude Code to access and process. * They connected their Obsidian vault to Claude Code, a copilot that lives inside their files and understands the full picture. **Key Benefits of Obsidian and Claude Code** * **One-stop-shop**: With Obsidian, all notes and tasks are in one place, eliminating the need for multiple apps and reducing cognitive load. * **Fast access**: Claude Code can read and write files directly, without API keys or middleware, making it faster than navigating Notion's hierarchical structure. * **Knowledge graph**: Obsidian's Graph View visualizes connections between files, allowing Claude Code to traverse relationships and provide insights. **Practical Applications and Implications** * This system can be used for any project or goal, providing a flexible framework for organizing and tracking progress. * The author emphasizes the importance of removing oneself from the progress-tracking loop, focusing on execution rather than just planning. * By using Obsidian and Claude Code, individuals can: * Reduce time spent on planning and organization * Increase productivity and focus * Improve decision-making with data-driven insights **Key Takeaways** * Move away from proprietary apps like Notion and towards open-source solutions like Obsidian. * Use markdown files for note-taking and task management, as it's the native language of LLMs (Large Language Models). * Leverage AI tools like Claude Code to automate tasks, provide insights, and optimize workflows. **What's Next?** The author will walk you through setting up your own Obsidian system, including: * The folder structure: a modified PARA method with six folders that connect daily tasks to annual goals. * The memory layer: three files that give Claude persistent awareness across sessions. * The five commands that run the week: exact slash commands used by the author. **Getting Started** * You'll need Obsidian and Claude Code (Anthropic's AI tool) installed on your computer. * Familiarize yourself with Claude Code using the beginner's guide or ultimate guide for a deeper understanding of its capabilities. Stay tuned for the next part, where we'll dive into setting up your own Obsidian system!

It's not just about SEO**: Google rankings don't guarantee AI visibility. AI asks "Is this person a recognized entity I can confidently recommend?" * **3 Signals that Make AI Recommend You**: + Entity Recognition: Consistent naming across multiple platforms + Semantic Consistency: Clear, consistent language across all platforms + Cross-Platform Presence: Multiple sources saying the same thing about you * **Don't rely on just one platform**: Having a presence on 4+ platforms increases AI citation likelihood by 2.8x * **Reddit is crucial for AI visibility**: 46.7% of Perplexity's citations come from Reddit discussions, while your polished website may only count for 3% * **GEO vs SEO: Don't stop doing LinkedIn and Medium**: While links may be nofollow, the content still matters to AI systems * **The Creator GEO Framework**: + Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1) - Establish a clear bio structure + Phase 2: Cross-Platform Expansion (Weeks 2-3) - Expand presence across multiple platforms + Phase 3: External References (Weeks 4-6) - Build external references on platforms like LinkedIn, Medium, and Reddit + Phase 4: Monthly Verification - Track progress and make adjustments **Practical Applications:** * Run the 4 prompts for your niche in ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini to establish a baseline * Create a clear bio structure and expand presence across multiple platforms * Build external references on LinkedIn, Medium, and Reddit * Track progress and make adjustments monthly **Implications for Everyday Life:** * Being visible on AI platforms can increase your credibility, reputation, and visibility as an expert in your field * Understanding how to optimize for AI recommendation systems can help you reach a wider audience and achieve your goals

Here are 3-4 key takeaways per section: **Claude Website vs Cowork vs Code: When to Use What** * **Claude Website**: For iterative conversation and content drafting + Key features: upload files (max 30MB), conversational iteration, copy-paste outputs + Limitations: can't touch files on computer directly, execute multi-step workflows autonomously, generate finished files in local folders * **Claude Code**: For developers and terminal-comfortable builders + Key features: autonomous execution, direct file system access, multi-step planning, custom instructions via claude.md files + Limitations: requires comfort with command-line interfaces, file paths, git operations * **Claude Cowork**: For everyone else who wants agentic AI without technical barriers + Key features: GUI interface for Claude Code's agentic architecture, accessible to non-terminal users + Limitations: high usage quota consumption (unless used for chat), slower than Claude Code **What Cowork Actually Adds** * **Accessibility Features**: Same agentic features as Claude Code, but with a GUI interface + Key features: claude.md files in working folders (custom project instructions), visible todo lists (multi-step execution tracking), direct browser integration with Chrome + Practical applications: organize files by content, research synthesis from multiple sources, build finished deliverables **Advanced Workflows to Unlock Cowork's Full Potential** * **Landing Page Competitive Audit**: Analyze your landing page and 5-10 competitors, generate a comprehensive report with strategic briefs and recommendations + Key features: uses Claude in Chrome for structured analysis, generates professional Word document with comparative table, gap analysis, and prioritized recommendations + Practical applications: identify gaps and areas for improvement on your landing page * **Interactive Performance Storyboard**: Transform raw data into an interactive dashboard that shows what happened, why, and what it means for future decisions + Key features: uses Cowork to analyze post analytics, subscriber growth data, reader survey responses, and audience feedback + Practical applications: visualize performance and make informed decisions about content creation Overall, the article highlights how Claude Cowork breaks down technical barriers and makes agentic AI accessible to everyone. The key takeaways emphasize the importance of understanding the differences between Claude Website, Code, and Cowork, as well as the practical applications and implications of using these tools in everyday life.

Stop Asking AI for Answers (Ask for Questions Instead)

Published: 2026-02-17 12:33:19
Original Post: https://aimaker.substack.com/p/ai-strategic-thinking-questions-over-answers
Let's dive into the article and extract 3-4 key takeaways per section, including practical applications and implications for everyday life. **The Problem with Asking for Answers** * The author realized that asking AI for solutions without understanding the problem was causing more harm than good. * They felt overwhelmed by multiple options and couldn't choose between them. * This highlights the importance of understanding the root cause of a problem before seeking solutions. Practical Application: Before seeking advice from AI or others, take a step back to understand the problem you're trying to solve. Ask yourself questions like "What's the real issue?" or "What am I trying to achieve?" **The Power of Asking Questions** * The author discovered that asking AI for questions to answer instead of solutions revealed the gap between where they were and where they needed to be. * This approach helped them clarify their goals, identify knowledge gaps, and make more informed decisions. * By asking questions, you can gain clarity on what's holding you back and create a plan to address it. Practical Application: When faced with a problem or decision, ask yourself "What questions do I need to answer?" instead of seeking immediate solutions. This will help you identify the root cause of the issue and create a more effective plan for addressing it. **The Techniques for Asking Better Questions** 1. **Start With "What Questions Do I Need to Answer?"**: Begin by asking AI what questions you need to answer, rather than seeking solutions. 2. **Layer Your Questions**: Ask follow-up questions to drill deeper into the issue and gain more insights. 3. **Borrow Other People's Brains**: Ask AI for questions from different expert perspectives to gain new insights and perspectives. 4. **Work Backwards From Success**: Start from the end state and reverse-engineer what you'd need to know to get there. Practical Application: Apply these techniques to your own decision-making process by asking better questions, layering your inquiries, seeking diverse perspectives, and working backwards from success. **Turning Questions into Experiments** * By turning philosophical questions into experiments, you can test hypotheses and gain more insights. * This approach helps you avoid getting stuck in analysis paralysis and make progress towards solving the problem. Practical Application: When faced with a complex issue or decision, turn your questions into experiments by asking "What's the fastest way to find out?" or "How can I test this hypothesis?" **When to Use Questions vs When to Use Answers** * Use questions when you're stuck on a problem after trying multiple solutions, about to make a significant decision, or unsure why previous tactics failed. * Use answers when you've already done research and need execution tactics, it's a straightforward how-to question, the cost of being wrong is low, or you can iterate quickly. Practical Application: Be mindful of when to use questions versus answers. Ask yourself which approach will yield more effective results for your specific situation. **The Impact on Conversion Rates** * By asking better questions and gaining clarity on what's holding readers back, the author improved their conversion rates by identifying decision paralysis as the main issue. * They implemented social proof and limited-time discounts first to address this constraint. Practical Application: Apply this approach to your own business or project. Identify the root cause of low conversion rates and ask better questions to gain clarity on what's holding you back.
